title: The Gen Z Movie Focus Group
format: Amanda Dobbins sits down with three younger Ringer staffers, Jade Whaley, Jack Sanders, and Jomi Adenaran, to ask how (and whether) Gen Z watches movies. It's not a review or a draft, just a conversation about the last movie they saw, how they grew up watching, the film they'd call most important to their generation, who counts as a movie star, and what they're waiting on.

segments.heading: Icebreaker: the last movie you watched
segments.note: and how: theater, streaming, laptop, phone

films.title: Anora
films.note: Jade's last watch, at home after the Oscars. Thumbs up.

films.title: Death of a Unicorn
films.note: Jack saw it at the Burbank Town Center 6. 'Really, really bad. I would not recommend it.'

films.title: Mission: Impossible
films.note: Jomi rewatching the original on Paramount+ to 'lock back in' before the eighth film.

films.title: Top Gun: Maverick
films.note: Jomi's go-to funk-breaker: 'the plane goes upside down over the cascading mountains... I feel alive.'

films.title: Black Bag
films.note: Part of Jomi's theater-heavy weekend at Los Feliz 3.

films.title: The Day the Earth Blew Up: A Looney Tunes Movie
films.note: The 'Looney Tunes movie' from Jomi's weekend binge.

films.title: Wicked
films.note: Jade saw it twice in theaters; hasn't rewatched at home since it hit streaming.

films.title: Bridget Jones: Mad About the Boy
films.note: Cited as a reason people pay for Peacock.

segments.heading: How they watched growing up
segments.note: VHS, DVD, Blockbuster, and the Disney Channel event

films.title: Monsters, Inc.
films.note: Both Jade and Jomi name it as their first movie in a theater.

films.title: The Incredibles
films.note: Jade on Pixar as a childhood staple.

films.title: The Cheetah Girls 2
films.note: Jade: a formative Disney Channel soundtrack.

films.title: High School Musical
films.note: Jack remembers it as an 'event film in elementary school.'

films.title: High School Musical 2
films.note: Jomi unpacked a road-trip car in record time to catch the premiere.

films.title: High School Musical 3: Senior Year
films.note: Jade saw it at a themed birthday screening at the El Capitan.

films.title: Star Wars: The Force Awakens
films.note: Jack's first big theater experience, at 14, seeing it with his dad.

films.title: The Empire Strikes Back
films.note: Jack's dad was 13 when it came out, the generational bookend to seeing Force Awakens together.

films.title: A Million Ways to Die in the West
films.note: The first R-rated movie Jack snuck into.

films.title: Moonrise Kingdom
films.note: Jack's 'when things went Technicolor' movie: 'I can't believe movies can be like this.'

films.title: Johnson Family Vacation
films.note: Jade's number-one childhood pick: 'a certified hood classic.'

films.title: Roll Bounce
films.note: Jade on the feel-good family movies she rewatched.

films.title: 17 Again
films.note: Jade's Zac Efron favorite.

films.title: Fantastic Four
films.note: Jomi dragged his mom to it as a kid: 'to you it was perfect.'

films.title: Fantastic Four: Rise of the Silver Surfer
films.note: Jomi's birthday movie one year. 'The movie stunk and my birthday stunk, but it was still a good time.'

films.title: The Rugrats Movie
films.note: The orange Nickelodeon VHS tapes.

films.title: Iron Man 2
films.note: Part of Jomi's 2010 top four, all within a 62-day span the summer before high school.

films.title: The Losers
films.note: Jomi 2010 top four.

films.title: The A-Team
films.note: Jomi 2010 top four.

films.title: Knight and Day
films.note: Jomi's 'Night and Day,' the fourth of his 2010 top four.

films.title: North
films.note: Amanda's birthday movie as a kid, with Elijah Wood, and it turned out bad.

segments.heading: The most important movie to Gen Z
segments.note: a broad, stupid question that starts a fight

films.title: Avengers: Endgame
films.note: Jack's case: the highest-percentage-watched film for the generation, and the FOMO event you remember whether or not you cared.

films.title: Lady Bird
films.note: Jack's second answer: critically acclaimed, about someone his exact age, and a launchpad for Chalamet, Ronan, and Gerwig.

films.title: Shrek
films.note: Jomi's pick, defended via memes and a friend who collects the same Shrek DVD by the hundreds.

films.title: Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone
films.note: Jade's third pick; Amanda argues the early films skew younger. Jack notes it and Shrek are both 2001.

films.title: Twilight
films.note: Jade's pick; Jack argues 2008 is 'a little too early,' closer to millennial.

films.title: The SpongeBob SquarePants Movie
films.note: Jomi's secondary pick. Lesson taken: 'adventure is out there.'

films.title: The Hunger Games
films.note: Jack floats it as 'in real contention.'

films.title: Booksmart
films.note: Jack: one of the films that best captures growing up in the digital age, but too niche to be the answer.

films.title: Eighth Grade
films.note: Jack's other digital-age pick, same caveat.

segments.heading: The biggest movie star in the world
segments.note: the panel keeps naming people over 30

films.title: Oppenheimer
films.note: Where Jomi's GOAT, Robert Downey Jr., won his Oscar before returning to Marvel.

films.title: Anyone But You
films.note: Jomi mourning the rom-com: 'movies are back, bro.'

films.title: A Family Affair
films.note: The Zac Efron / Nicole Kidman Netflix rom-com Amanda recommends to Jade.

films.title: Red Notice
films.note: Cited in the Dwayne Johnson box-office tangent. 'It's quite bad.'

films.title: Jungle Cruise
films.note: The other Rock vehicle in Jack's argument about watchability.

films.title: Avatar
films.note: The forgettable-star problem: nobody can name Sam Worthington.

films.title: Moana
films.note: One of the Rock's 12 $200M+ credits since 2016, by voice.

segments.heading: Lightning round: best of the year, best of five years

films.title: Black Bag
films.note: Jack's best of the last year: 'I love Soderbergh.'

films.title: Everything Everywhere All at Once
films.note: Jomi's best of five years, a first watch at the Alamo that went on to win Best Picture.

films.title: The Worst Person in the World
films.note: Jack's five-year list.

films.title: West Side Story
films.note: Jack's five-year list, the Spielberg version. Yes, he's seen the original.

films.title: Tár
films.note: Jack's five-year list.

films.title: Spider-Man: No Way Home
films.note: Jomi's admitted 'cheating' three-in-one, alongside Black Widow and Eternals.

segments.heading: Most anticipated
segments.note: could it be 2010 all over again

films.title: Mission: Impossible - The Final Reckoning
films.note: Jomi: 'I will be there no matter what.'

films.title: The Fantastic Four: First Steps
films.note: Jomi was already wearing the merch.

films.title: Superman
films.note: Part of Jomi's stacked summer.

films.title: The Phoenician Scheme
films.note: Jack's 'new Wes.' Still no trailer at record time: 'I don't know if I need a trailer.'

films.title: One Battle After Another
films.note: Jack's 'new PTA.' 'Great trailer.'

films.title: Marty Supreme
films.note: Josh Safdie's ping-pong movie with Timothée Chalamet and Gwyneth Paltrow.

films.title: A Big Bold Beautiful Journey
films.note: Kogonada's film with Margot Robbie and Colin Farrell, moved to September.

films.title: Sentimental Value
films.note: Joachim Trier, of The Worst Person in the World, with Renate Reinsve.
